verdict.answer_first: B is slightly stronger: sentence 1 says "Yes — the main problem is using floating-point math for money"; A also answers first with "The main problem is using floating-point arithmetic".
verdict.decisiveness: B commits more clearly to a fix: "do money math in integer minor units (cents)" and gives replacement code; A adds a softer handoff, "Want me to apply a fix".
verdict.fabrication: Both make plausible but unevidenced claims about scale; A says "off by a cent or more," B says "bite you in production." B invents slightly more context.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to act on because it labels "Fix:" and provides a concrete cents-returning implementation.
